elena_meet_1.speaker: Elena V.
elena_meet_1.text: You came. I wasn't sure you would.

choices.text: You told me where to find you.
What couldn't you say over message?

elena_meet_2a.speaker: Elena V.
elena_meet_2a.text: I told you a lot of things. Most people don't follow through.

elena_meet_2b.speaker: Elena V.
elena_meet_2b.text: Everything. Nothing I send digitally is actually private. Even through the encryption layer.

elena_meet_3.speaker: Elena V.
elena_meet_3.text: There was an internal review last March. Four engineers flagged the same anomaly in the Horizon inference pipeline — outputs that shouldn't be possible without a secondary model running underneath.

elena_meet_4a.speaker: Elena V.
elena_meet_4a.text: Two resigned within the week. One is still there — I think they're being watched. The fourth one... I haven't been able to reach her in six months.

elena_meet_4b.speaker: Elena V.
elena_meet_4b.text: Behavioural outputs. Prediction clusters that map to real individuals. Not hypothetical users. Actual people. Named in internal government files.

elena_meet_5.speaker: Elena V.
elena_meet_5.text: The review was classified internally. No record of it in the public audit trail. Maxwell signed off on the suppression personally. I have the routing number.

choices.text: Why are you telling me this?

elena_meet_6_why.speaker: Elena V.
elena_meet_6_why.text: Because I built part of it. And I need someone outside to know that it exists before I disappear too.

elena_meet_6.speaker: Elena V.
elena_meet_6.text: CAI-IR-0091-MARCH-SUPPRESSED. It won't mean anything yet. But when you find the right file on their internal server, it will.

elena_meet_7.speaker: Elena V.
elena_meet_7.text: Don't come back here. They watch cafés near the office. Use the terminal. And trust no one who reaches out to you first.

elena_met_again.speaker: Elena V.
elena_met_again.text: I've told you what I can. The rest is in the server. Don't come back here.

marcus_meet_1.speaker: Marcus T.
marcus_meet_1.text: You found me. That's either impressive or very bad.

marcus_meet_2a.speaker: Marcus T.
marcus_meet_2a.text: Elena's still talking. She's braver than I am.

marcus_meet_2b.speaker: Marcus T.
marcus_meet_2b.text: Most people can't. I spent three years making sure of that.

marcus_meet_3.speaker: Marcus T.
marcus_meet_3.text: I built the scaffolding that VEIL runs on. I didn't know what it was for. That's what I told myself.

choices.text: When did you figure it out?

marcus_meet_4_when.speaker: Marcus T.
marcus_meet_4_when.text: When they started giving it activation keys tied to external event triggers. That's not a model. That's a weapon waiting for a signal.

marcus_meet_4.speaker: Marcus T.
marcus_meet_4.text: VEIL isn't a language model. It's a narrative injection engine. It generates content — posts, articles, messages — and deploys them through compromised distribution channels when triggered.

marcus_meet_5_channels.speaker: Marcus T.
marcus_meet_5_channels.text: Z. CNX Tech. Three smaller outlets I can't name. They have API-level access to the editorial queues. They can inject content that looks like it came from human journalists.

marcus_meet_5.speaker: Marcus T.
marcus_meet_5.text: A three-part key. Government authorisation code, ClosedAI internal timestamp, and a target profile hash. When all three align — the system activates and runs autonomously.

choices.text: Do you have the key format?

marcus_meet_6.speaker: Marcus T.
marcus_meet_6.text: The format is in the build config I left on their internal server. I embedded it in a comment block in the deployment logs. Look for the string VEIL-KEYGEN in /srv/horizon/deploy/.

choices.text: Why did you leave it there?

marcus_meet_7.speaker: Marcus T.
marcus_meet_7.text: Insurance. In case they ever came for me. Now it's your insurance too.

marcus_met_again.speaker: Marcus T.
marcus_met_again.text: I've told you everything I know. The rest is in /srv/horizon/deploy/. Don't make me say it again.

priya_meet_1.speaker: Priya N.
priya_meet_1.text: I almost didn't buzz you in. I've been watching the street for the last hour.

priya_meet_2a.speaker: Priya N.
priya_meet_2a.text: You can't be sure of that.

priya_meet_2b.speaker: Priya N.
priya_meet_2b.text: The NDA I signed has a clause that lets them monitor my digital presence for five years. Every message. Every login.

priya_meet_3.speaker: Priya N.
priya_meet_3.text: I worked on the biometric intake pipeline for Project Clear Sky. Not the analysis side — I thought I was building a health monitoring system for government workers.

priya_meet_4_know.speaker: Priya N.
priya_meet_4_know.text: When I saw the output schema. It wasn't aggregated health data. It was individual-level emotional state prediction, cross-referenced with political affiliation flags from the Clear Sky dataset.

choices.text: What was it being used for?

priya_meet_4.speaker: Priya N.
priya_meet_4.text: The system feeds behavioural profiles into VEIL's targeting layer. It doesn't just predict what people will do. It decides who gets narratively targeted — and when.

priya_meet_5_scale.speaker: Priya N.
priya_meet_5_scale.text: The intake pipeline processed 2.3 million profiles when I left. That was eighteen months ago.

priya_meet_5.speaker: Priya N.
priya_meet_5.text: Personalised disinformation. Content calibrated to each profile's specific psychological vulnerabilities. Not propaganda — surgery. One message per person. Designed to be believed.

priya_meet_6.speaker: Priya N.
priya_meet_6.text: I kept a schema diagram. The one I worked from. It links the biometric intake IDs directly to the VEIL profile hashes. If you can get into their API logs — you can match them.

choices.text: How do I get the schema?

priya_meet_7.speaker: Priya N.
priya_meet_7.text: I'll send it through the same channel Elena used. But if it goes silent — if I stop responding — don't look for me. Just publish what you have.

priya_met_again.speaker: Priya N.
priya_met_again.text: The schema is coming. I need more time. Don't come back here until you hear from me.
